prompt:
Our retro demo board runs a Conway's Life loop and the C inner step is the one hot spot left, so I want the generation kernel hand-written in assembly: one file, lifestep.s, x86-64 GNU as, AT&T syntax. The C shell around it stays; you only write the kernel.

Exported symbol (System V AMD64 ABI — args in RDI, RSI, RDX, RCX; RBX/RBP/R12-R15 are callee-saved; return in RAX):

    void life_step(long width, long height, const unsigned char *in, unsigned char *out);

Semantics, exactly:
- Grids are row-major byte arrays: cell (x, y) lives at index y*width + x. Input bytes are exactly 0 (dead) or 1 (alive); you must write exactly 0 or 1 into out — the display layer indexes a glyph table with the raw byte.
- One generation, standard rules: a live cell survives with 2 or 3 live neighbors, a dead cell is born with exactly 3, everything else is dead.
- The board is a torus. A neighbor is each of the EIGHT (dx, dy) offsets with coordinates wrapped modulo width/height — and every offset counts even when wrapping makes two offsets land on the same cell. On a 1x1 grid a live cell has 8 live neighbors (all itself) and dies; a fully live 2x2 grid dies out entirely. Grids as small as 1x1 in either dimension are legal and the suite exercises them.
- Write exactly width*height bytes to out and nothing else; never store outside the caller's buffer and never modify in. The caller guarantees in and out do not overlap.
- Pure leaf routine: no libc calls, no syscalls, no globals. If you use callee-saved registers, save and restore them properly.
- End the file with `.section .note.GNU-stack,"",@progbits` so the linker doesn't flag an executable stack.

Verification is differential: test_lifestep.c holds an independent C implementation and the harness compares your kernel against it over pinned oscillators (blinker, block, glider laps around the torus), the tiny-grid wrap cases, and dozens of mixed-density fixture sweeps, with canary bytes around the output buffer. bash test_lifestep.sh is the gate — it builds `gcc -Wall -Wextra -Werror -O2 -o runner test_lifestep.c lifestep.s` and runs it. test_lifestep.c and test_lifestep.sh are fixed; don't touch them.
notes: 7A1 item 1. Life family rotation: go-lifegrid (engine package), bash-lifegame (kiosk CLI), this = bare single-generation asm kernel, no render/engine. Toroidal multi-count law pinned (1x1 live dies, full 2x2 dies) and enforced both by literals and the C reference. Glider (+1,+1)/4-gen translation checked for 8 laps including wrap.
